title: The Docket - Week 5a (Friday Edition)
subject: The $4.2B legal AI market just got its first real scandal
preheader: A vendor was caught training on client data without consent. Names inside.
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Friday Edition — May 8, 2026

This one couldn’t wait until Wednesday. A legal AI vendor just got caught doing the one thing they all promise they don’t do.

🚨 Legal AI Vendor Caught Training on Client Data

A mid-market contract analysis platform — we’re not naming them yet because the investigation is ongoing — was caught using client-uploaded contracts to train its models, despite its privacy policy explicitly stating it wouldn’t. The discovery came when a firm noticed that the tool’s suggestions for a highly confidential deal included language that was nearly identical to provisions from a different client’s contract that had been uploaded months earlier. The firm retained a forensic AI firm to investigate, and the results were damning. This is the legal AI industry’s first major trust breach, and it validates every concern skeptics have raised.

🛡️ What to Ask Your AI Vendor Right Now

In light of this news, here are three questions every firm should be asking their AI vendors today: (1) Is any client data used for model training, fine-tuning, or improvement? Get it in writing. (2) Can you provide a SOC 2 report and a data processing agreement? (3) What happens to uploaded data after the session ends — is it retained, and for how long? If your vendor can’t answer these clearly and in writing, reconsider the relationship. Our vendor assessment guide has a full checklist.

📈 Despite Scandal, Legal AI Adoption Accelerates

Ironically, the same week as this trust breach, a Gartner report showed that legal AI tool adoption grew 78% year-over-year. The appetite isn’t slowing down. But this scandal will — and should — shift the conversation from “should we adopt AI?” to “how do we adopt AI safely?” That’s actually a healthier conversation.

Tool Spotlight

Our Tools: Built With Privacy First

Every tool on LegalTech AI Hub — Clause Checker, Doc Diff, Argument Gap Analyzer — processes data locally or with zero retention. Nothing you upload is stored, trained on, or accessible after your session ends. We built it this way because we knew this day would come.

Try Our Free Tools →

We’ll have more on this story as it develops. Ask your vendors the hard questions this week. Don’t wait.
